title: Elements must only use supported ARIA attributes
summary: Ensure an element's role supports its ARIA attributes
notes: Applied the recommended fix to the element

code.title: Elements must only use supported ARIA attributes
code.summary: Ensure an element's role supports its ARIA attributes

html.before: <input type="text" id="twotabsearchtextbox" value="" name="field-keywords" autocomplete="off" placeholder="Search Amazon.in" class="nav-input nav-progre..." dir="auto" tabindex="0" aria-label="Search Amazon.in" role="sea
html.after:
<!-- Fix applied to #twotabsearchtextbox -->
<!-- Elements must only use supported ARIA attributes -->
<input type="text" id="twotabsearchtextbox" value="" name="field-keywords" autocomplete="off" placeholder="Search Amazon.in" class="nav-input nav-progre..." dir="auto" tabindex="0" aria-label="Search Amazon.in" role="sea

code.what_changed: Applied the recommended fix to the affected element.
code.why: Ensure an element's role supports its ARIA attributes

improvement_summary: The redesigned version fixes 'Elements must only use supported ARIA attributes'. Ensure an element's role supports its ARIA attributes This addresses a critical barrier and improves conformance with WCAG 4.1.2.
highlights: Resolves a critical issue mapped to WCAG 4.1.2
